title: Chronic Diarrhea and Poor Growth - Celiac Disease
chief_complaint: 3-year-old with chronic diarrhea (foul-smelling, greasy stools), abdominal distension, poor weight gain (dropped from 50th to 5th percentile), and irritability for 6 months. Symptoms started after introduction of wheat.

physical_exam.growth: Weight: 10.5 kg (<5th percentile), Height: 85 cm (10th percentile)
physical_exam.general: Wasted buttocks, thin limbs, protuberant abdomen

hidden_data.duodenal_biopsy: Villous atrophy with crypt hyperplasia and intraepithelial lymphocytosis (Marsh 3b)

key_learning_points: Anti-tTG IgA is screening test of choice

patient_responses.diarrhea: Her stools are pale, greasy, and smell terrible
patient_responses.growth: She was growing well until we started wheat

feedback.correct_message: Well diagnosed! Celiac disease with classic malabsorption.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is celiac disease. The positive antibodies + biopsy confirm it.
feedback.attending_note: Celiac disease: Strict gluten-free diet for life. Refer to dietitian. Monitor growth, tTG levels annually. Check iron, vitamin D, B12. Screen family members. Osteoporosis prevention. Most children show rapid improvement within weeks of GFD.
